Brigadier generals (com- modores) command   multi regiment forces and small space fleets Major generals (rear admirals command entire military regions (usually dozen or more planets), while Iieutenant gen- erals (vice admirals) command several military regions_ Generals admirals) command House entire armed lorce_
The com GuARDS Officially founded in 2933 , the ComStar Guards and Militla give the usual organizationa structure unlque twist  Rather than the standard lances , companies , battalions and s0 on , the Com Guards' organization is based on the number six.  This system, while different on the surface, translates easily Into standard terms The smallest Com Guard formation Is the Level unit; composed of single 'Mech, vehicle or Infantry platoon: Level unit consists of six Level units, effectively demi- company: Level unit; made up of six Level Ils. the same Size as battalion: The Level IV unit, commonly called divi- sion; made up of six Level Ills, or the equivalent of two stan- dard regiments Six Level IV units normally compose Level V unit, or army: Following the huge losses on Tukayyid and the secession of the Word of Blake, ComStar armies currently com- prise only four divisions apiece. ComStar fields grand total of twelve armies; the Word of Blake tields ten divisions The Com Guards use combined-arms rather than standard unit composition, which segregates difterent types of equip- ment into separate units_ The Com Guards use combined-arms composition to surprise and defeat unprepared  enemies. defensive operations, which account for most of their assign- ments the Com Guards have an unmatched ability t0 adapt t0 the constantly changing nature of the battlefield.
TRAINING Militaries rely o several different methods t0 train their soldiers The simplest and cheapest are boot camps -facili- ties that train raw recruits in the fundamentals of infantry com- bat and teach them basic military skills Some of the more advanced camps also train them to operate tanks and other combat vehicles: Each of these camps can turn out hundreds Or thousands of soldiers per year; SO every major military (as well as quite few larger mercenary units) operates at least one camp_ Boot-camp graduates are often committed to only few years 0f service. Though enlisted soldiers are easy to train (ne same can not be said for officers BattleMech and acrospace pilots_ These soldiers take years t0 train; ignificantly higher cost. Each major armed force relies on one or more military acade mies t0 produce these valuable personnel, usually requiring minimum service commitment of four t0 six years_ Private acad emies exist throughout the Inner Sphere_ but tuition is often exorbitant: Admission to ay of these academies very Com petitive At the end of the course of study: graduates receive degree and military commission: Though academy graduates aremuch sought-after; tney make up the minority of MechWarriors and aerospace pilots_ most of whom enter a apprenticeship program. Many merce nary commands offer such apprenticeships, while more affluent families hire private tutors train their children _ Tne course 0f study long: often starting at the age of twelve or thirteen and
continuing for six or more years. The apprenticeship method uses hands on approach to training and produces pilot who knows his or ner machine inside ad out. Those who complete these apprenticeships usually enter service with mercenary unit, though every House military will take apprenticed pilots and MechWarriors.
METHODS OF WAR Though each House military once boasted the ability to place full BattleMech regiment ad dozen Or more support regiments on nearly every inhabited world_ those days are long gone After the ravages of the First Succession Wal, generals were torced t0 aujust t0 different style of fighting- Throughout the Succession Wars, the Successor States continued t0 vie for control of important worlds ad their indus tres Unable to field divisions ad corps in these operations; commanders often nad t0 rely on single regiment t0 take their objective _ usually battling defending forces of equal lesser size . When large forces did clash, they rarely tought the death. Units that were clearly outmatched with no chance of safe withdrawal simply capitulated_ The commander 0f the vic torious force would then allow the defeated force t0 withdraw from the planet exchange for ransom; usually lance of equipment per battalion allowed to retreat;: Objective raids became popular substitute for planetary assaults during the Succession Wars With the costs of plane-
